items.headline: Historic house preservation plan allows four new townhomes
items.summary: A heritage resource at 434 Crescent Avenue would be modified to retain the original 960-square foot house while adding four three-story townhomes in the rear. The project balances preservation of the historic structure with infill development.

items.headline: City Council approves digital signage agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects what advertising and displays residents will see on municipal buildings and property throughout San José.

items.headline: City seeking proposals for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The council received a report on requests for proposals to contract encampment cleanup services. This indicates the city is planning a new or expanded approach to addressing homeless encampments in San José.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: The council approved actions related to replacing an elevator at an unnamed terminal facility. Details on cost, timeline, and location would affect residents depending on which transit or public facility is involved.

items.headline: City negotiating purchase of Finch Avenue property with school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District to buy or secure terms on the 10480 Finch Avenue property. The outcome could affect future school or city facilities in that area.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a comprehensive master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area, which will guide development of new recreational spaces and connectivity in that part of Milpitas. This plan shapes what green spaces and pedestrian/bike infrastructure residents can expect in coming years.

items.headline: El Paseo De Saratoga breaks ground on 772-unit mixed-use development
items.summary: Two major apartment buildings totaling 772 units and $125.7M in investment are launching at El Paseo De Saratoga. This substantial housing project addresses San José's ongoing affordability crisis and represents significant urban infill development.

items.headline: 260-unit affordable housing complex approved at Berryessa
items.summary: Berryessa Family Apartments will add 260 new units ($52.4M) to a key transit corridor. This major workforce housing project expands the city's affordable inventory in an underutilized area.
